title: Current loops act as unintended antennas
description: Radiated emissions scale with loop area and frequency, so tight current loops are the primary EMI control in layout.
body: Use when reasoning about where a board's emissions come from. Do not think of EMI as purely a shielding problem, because the radiating loop is created during routing. Actions: identify the high-current and high-frequency loops, minimize their enclosed area, and inspect clock and switching paths. Validation signals include small loop areas on critical nets and low radiated levels at clock harmonics in a pre-scan. Risk boundary: a large high-frequency loop can fail emissions outright, so flag any wide return loop for tightening before fabrication.
